description: Fix the double-prompt-building bug in callLLM() and synthesizeWithToolResult(). Currently, the orchestrator carefully builds a token-budgeted prompt via MKContextBuilder, then passes it to cloudLLM->generateWithContext() which IGNORES that pre-built prompt and rebuilds its own from scratch (appending facts and history again). The fix is to use cloudLLM->generate(userInput, prompt) instead, which sends the pre-built prompt directly as the system prompt.
steps: In MK_OS/orchestrator/conversation_loop.cpp, locate the callLLM() method (around line 195 in the 'Fall back to cloud LLM' section). Replace the call: cloudLLM->generateWithContext(input, facts, history, "", prompt) with cloudLLM->generate(input, prompt). The generate() method in cloud_llm.cpp takes (userPrompt, systemPrompt) and sends systemPrompt as the system message and userPrompt as the user message. Since 'prompt' already contains the full assembled prompt (system + tools + facts + history + 'User: input\nMK:'), pass the assembled prompt as the systemPrompt and pass input as the userPrompt. This way the LLM gets the full context without double-building.

In the same file, locate synthesizeWithToolResult() method (around line 230). It builds its own prompt string manually, then calls cloudLLM->generateWithContext(). Replace that cloudLLM->generateWithContext() call with cloudLLM->generate(input, prompt) where 'prompt' is the manually assembled string that already contains facts, history, tool results, etc. The local LLM path (llmEngine->generate(prompt)) is already correct - just fix the cloud path.
acceptance_criteria: callLLM() uses cloudLLM->generate() instead of cloudLLM->generateWithContext()
synthesizeWithToolResult() uses cloudLLM->generate() instead of cloudLLM->generateWithContext()
The project compiles cleanly with the build command
verification: Run: cd MK_OS && g++ -std=c++17 -O2 -Wall -DLINUX -o mk_os system/mk_entry.cpp -lcurl -lsqlite3
findings: Build compiles cleanly (zero errors, only pre-existing warnings). All 96 tests pass. The paper_trading_buy_deducts_fees test may appear flaky on first run due to stale SQLite state from previous test executions but passes on clean re-run.
